items.title: SA Police investigating after man becomes unresponsive during Clare arrest

children.content: Police are investigating an incident at Clare, in South Australia's mid north, where a man became unresponsive during an arrest.

gallery.altText: A close up image of an SA Police car.

thumbnail.altText: A close up image of an SA Police car.

items.title: 'Beyond our capacity' to defend sea trade lanes, Shoebridge says

children.content: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

image.altText: David Shoebridge wears blue suit jacket, pink shirt and no tie. Speaking in a corridor to the media.

items.title: 30 years of Pokémon: The 'immersive' phenomenon still finding new fans

children.content: Three decades after the franchise that coined the phrase "Gotta catch 'em all!" was first released, parents are sharing their love of the franchise with their children.

image.altText: A man in a pokemon hat with his son with pokemon toys and photos

items.title: Homelessness service concerned by changing demographic in clients

children.content: As cost of living and housing pressures intensify, a South Australian homelessness service says more young women and single mothers are reaching out for support.

gallery.altText: A person sleeping wrapped in a blanket in the middle of a grassy reserve, city streets and cars in the background

thumbnail.altText: A person sleeping wrapped in a blanket in the middle of a grassy reserve, city streets and cars in the background

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

image.altText: An elderly woman holding an oval framed photo of a young child wearing Elizabethan costume

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

image.altText: Port Adelaide players hug and and congratulate each other after a win

items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit sits on a chair inside looking at the camera with a neutral expression.

items.title: Public servant claims threat of $4m damages bill in golf course fight

children.content: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Mother creates modest activewear to empower herself and others

children.content: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

gallery.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

items.title: Future of iconic wine Wolf Blass unclear amid large brand overhaul

children.content: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

image.altText: a red wine label

items.title: Body-in-suitcase killer loses bid to reduce sentence for double murder

children.content: The NSW Court of Criminal Appeal dismisses a bid by Daniel Holdom to have his sentence reduced after he pleaded guilty in 2018 to murdering his girlfriend and her two-year-old daughter before dumping the child's body in a suitcase on the side of a remote highway.

image.altText: Khandalyce Kiara Pearce and Karlie Jade Pearce-Stevenson

items.title: Honouring the man who kept cinema alive in remote outback city

children.content: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

image.altText: A black and white photograph of a man dressed up as Rambo, posing with a gun

items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

image.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

items.title: 'Sick and tired': Northern Adelaide hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours

children.content: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

image.altText: A lady in a black t-shirt stands with arms folded defiantly out front of the entrance to Lyell McEwin Hospital

items.title: Full Circle launches professional musical theatre company in Adelaide

children.content: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

image.altText: Woman sits on a chair in a garden

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Psychologist sounds alarm over looksmaxxing and 'out-mogging'

children.content: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

image.altText: A young man's face. He has dark hair. He looks straight ahead. There is a "web" of triangles in light green across his face.

items.title: Tasmania's police minister apologises for naming Port Arthur gunman

children.content: Felix Ellis has apologised for comments he made during budget estimates about the 1996 Port Arthur massacre in which he named the gunman, with one survivor labelling his comments "hurtful" and "disrespectful".

image.altText: A bearded man seen in profile.

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

image.altText: police albany 3

items.title: Struggling and desperate single mum saved by an 'employment mentor'

children.content: Personalised support helped domestic violence survivor and single mother Harsimran get a job, despite significant barriers. It's an approach that could benefit more jobseekers following the federal government's revamp of Workforce Australia.

gallery.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

thumbnail.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's streets

children.content: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

image.altText: Abhijeet Dipke, head of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), speaks to supporters of the CJP

items.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy  meeting

children.content: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

image.altText: A plume of black smoke is seen over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia,

items.title: 'It's not normal to be winning the way I'm winning': Johnson excelling in open water

children.content: Moesha Johnson is enjoying an overseas open water swimming adventure that has seen her win gold at every stage. But these are all just stepping stones for the biggest prize of all at the Los Angeles Olympics in 2028.

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas

children.content: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

image.altText: Diana Ordonez runs with her arms out

items.title: Pope competes with Bad Bunny in Spain, urges leaders to reject polarisation

children.content: Pope Leo urges global leaders not to "fan the flames of polarisation" to gain popularity, warning of the artificial environment created by new technologies.

image.altText: Pope Leo stands behind protective glass in the popemobile as it moved through a crowded street escorted by security

items.title: Planning, packing and nerves: Six Aussies kick off Race Around The World

children.content: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

children.content: CDs, DVDs, Blu-Rays and even VHS cassettes are enjoying a resurgence. Why are some returning to physical media?

items.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? It's time to show off your skills

children.content: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

items.title: 'We were so close': Laos cave rescue called off as conditions deteriorate

children.content: The search for two men trapped in a flooded cave in Laos has been postponed as rescuers say a "miracle" is needed.
